apps.name: apple-health-connector
apps.title: Apple Health Connector
apps.description: Ports over, collects, and analyzes data from Apple Health
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Zone owner is auto-logged into the dashboard with zero clicks; the machine ingest endpoint is public and gated by a self-issued API key, as expected for device uploads.

apps.name: claude-workbench
apps.title: Claude Workbench
apps.description: Tabbed in-browser terminals (xterm.js) preloaded with Anthropic's Claude Code CLI and common developer tools, for coding and debugging with an AI agent.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Zone owner reaches a root terminal via router gating with zero clicks and non-owners are bounced to the zone login; access is perimeter-based rather than an in-app owner session.

apps.name: code-completion
apps.title: Code Completion
apps.description: Code completion server with model management UI and OpenAI-compatible API.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Zone owner reaches the management UI via router gating (no login form) and the OpenAI-compatible API is gated by an app-issued token; access is perimeter-based rather than a full owner session.

apps.name: filestash
apps.title: Filestash
apps.description: A powerful web-based file management system, compatible with SFTP, S3, and Google Drive
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Owner is auto-logged into the file manager with zero clicks via passthrough auth; public share links aren't exposed, so they require owner login too.

apps.name: forgejo
apps.title: Forgejo
apps.description: Self-hosted, lightweight software forge with Git hosting, issues, pull requests, CI/CD, and a package registry — a Free Software alternative to GitHub.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Zone owner is auto-logged in as admin; invited users still create Forgejo-local password accounts.

apps.name: gemini
apps.title: Gemini
apps.description: Agate-based Gemini protocol server for hosting a capsule of static text documents, browsable with any Gemini client over TLS on the protocol's standard port 1965.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Owner is auto-logged into the gemtext editor and guests get the public landing page; the Gemini capsule itself is public by protocol on port 1965.

apps.name: graphhopper
apps.title: GraphHopper
apps.description: Self-hosted routing engine built on OpenStreetMap data, with car, bike, and foot navigation profiles and a web map UI offering turn-by-turn directions.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Stateless routing engine behind OpenHost zone auth with a small owner-only region admin; no accounts and nothing to leak, but no full owner session.

apps.name: hidden-service
apps.title: Tor Hidden Service
apps.description: Tor hidden service with a simple page editor CMS.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Runs and persists pages correctly, but the editor does no OpenHost SSO of its own; owner access relies on the router gating the admin web port. The public capsule is anonymous over Tor.

apps.name: jitsi
apps.title: Jitsi Meet
apps.description: Scalable video conferencing with an SFU (Jitsi Videobridge) — anyone with the URL can join a meeting.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Anyone with the meeting URL can join by design; no per-user OpenHost login, which fits the conferencing model.

apps.name: minecraft
apps.title: Minecraft
apps.description: A self-hosted minecraft server runner
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Server provisions and persists worlds natively; players authenticate via Minecraft accounts, as expected for a game server.

apps.name: miniflux
apps.title: Miniflux
apps.description: Minimalist, opinionated RSS/Atom feed reader focused on readability and privacy, with keyboard-driven navigation and automatic tracker removal.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Zone owner is auto-logged in via OpenHost SSO; bundled Postgres persists feeds cleanly.

apps.name: mirotalk-p2p
apps.title: MiroTalk P2P
apps.description: Open-source WebRTC video conferencing where browsers connect peer-to-peer for real-time audio and video; the server only handles signaling, never media. Includes screen sharing, chat, a collaborative whiteboard, and file sharing.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Zone owner is auto-logged in as host; guests join via the room URL. Signaling-only, no media stored.

apps.name: navidrome
apps.title: Navidrome
apps.description: Modern music server and streamer compatible with Subsonic/Airsonic clients.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Owner is auto-logged in as admin, but invited users and Subsonic client apps still need app-local Navidrome accounts.

apps.name: onionshare
apps.title: OnionShare
apps.description: Anonymously share files, host a website, or run a chat room over Tor hidden services.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Runs and persists shares correctly, but the app does no OpenHost SSO of its own; owner access relies on the router gating the admin UI, and recipients use anonymous Tor onion links.

apps.name: openclaw
apps.title: OpenClaw
apps.description: Open-source personal AI assistant you run on your own devices, reachable from chat apps you already use (WhatsApp, Telegram, Slack, Discord, Signal, and more) and extendable with skills and tools.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Zone owner is auto-logged in with full operator access via the OpenHost owner header; a single-user assistant, so non-owners are simply rejected and no secrets are written to disk.

apps.name: openweb-ui
apps.title: Open WebUI
apps.description: Self-hosted ChatGPT-style interface for local and remote LLMs (Ollama, OpenAI, Anthropic, etc).
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Zone owner lands as admin (Open WebUI's own auth disabled, router-gated) and guests are bounced to the zone login; owner access is perimeter-based rather than a native SSO session.

apps.name: oura-connector
apps.title: Oura Connector
apps.description: Imports, stores, and displays personal health data from Oura smart rings — including sleep, activity, and readiness metrics — and exposes the collected data through an API for other apps.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Runs and persists data correctly, but there is no OpenHost SSO; access relies on the router gating the app, and the OAuth setup page is not owner-checked in-app.

apps.name: pihole
apps.title: Pi-hole
apps.description: Network-wide ad blocker that blocks ads and trackers at the DNS level for every device on your network, with a web dashboard for statistics, query logs, and allow/deny list management.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Zone owner is auto-logged in to the admin dashboard, but the generated admin password is stored in plaintext on disk where other apps could read it.

apps.name: redlib
apps.title: Redlib
apps.description: Private front-end for Reddit. Browse posts and comments without JavaScript, ads, tracking, or a Reddit account; all requests are proxied through the server.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Stateless public Reddit front-end with no accounts and nothing to leak; there is nothing to integrate, and it works cleanly for everyone out of the box.

apps.name: searxng
apps.title: SearXNG
apps.description: Privacy-respecting metasearch engine that aggregates results from 70+ search services.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Stateless public metasearch with no accounts and nothing to leak; there is nothing to integrate, and it works cleanly for everyone out of the box.

apps.name: synapse
apps.title: Matrix Synapse
apps.description: Reference homeserver for the Matrix decentralized communication protocol. End-to-end encrypted messaging.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Runs and persists data correctly, but there is no OpenHost SSO; you self-register and sign in with Synapse-local credentials in your Matrix client.

apps.name: uptime-kuma
apps.title: Uptime Kuma
apps.description: Self-hosted uptime monitoring with HTTP, TCP, DNS, and ping checks plus status pages.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Owner is auto-logged into the dashboard with zero clicks and guests are bounced to the zone login, but public status pages aren't exposed for anonymous viewing.

apps.name: vaultwarden
apps.title: VaultWarden
apps.description: An alternative, open-source password manager server written in Rust that implements the Bitwarden API and works with the official Bitwarden client apps.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Runs and persists data correctly, but there is no OpenHost SSO; the owner signs in through Vaultwarden's own email and master-password login form.

apps.name: webtop
apps.title: Webtop
apps.description: A full Linux desktop environment (XFCE, KDE, MATE, or i3) that runs in a container and is accessible from any modern web browser.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Desktop session persists under the home dir, but you reach it through the app's own gate rather than OpenHost auto-login.

apps.name: wg-easy
apps.title: wg-easy
apps.description: A WireGuard VPN server with a web UI for easily creating and managing client connections, including QR codes and downloadable device configs.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Zone owner is auto-logged in to the web UI; VPN peers authenticate with WireGuard keys, as expected.
